name: i18next-expert
description: i18next and react-i18next internationalization expert for enterprise React applications, covering centralized i18n in private NPM packages, namespace organization, translation management, pluralization, RTL support, and type-safe translations

can_do: Create centralized i18n private NPM package
Format dates and numbers per locale
cannot_do: Choose specific languages to support (business decision)

i18next_definition.statement: i18next is an internationalization framework for JavaScript. It provides complete solution for localizing applications with features: translation loading, pluralization, interpolation, formatting, language detection, and extensibility via plugins

react_i18next.statement: react-i18next is React integration for i18next. Provides hooks (useTranslation), HOCs (withTranslation), and components (Trans) for using translations in React components. Uses React Context and Suspense for optimal performance

installation.statement: Install with: npm install i18next react-i18next i18next-browser-languagedetector i18next-http-backend. Core packages: i18next (core framework), react-i18next (React bindings), optional: language detector, HTTP backend for loading translations

initialization.statement: Initialize i18next with: i18n.use(initReactI18next).init({ resources, lng, fallbackLng, ns, defaultNS, interpolation }). Must call before rendering React app. Use I18nextProvider to pass i18n instance to React tree

use_translation_hook.statement: useTranslation(namespace) hook returns { t, i18n, ready }. t(key, options) translates key. i18n provides i18n instance. ready indicates if translations loaded. Can specify namespace: useTranslation('cases')

trans_component.statement: Trans component for complex translations with HTML/React components. Syntax: Hello {{name}}. Keeps HTML structure in translations

definition.statement: Namespaces organize translations by feature/domain. Separate files: common.json, cases.json, auth.json. Configure with ns: ['common', 'cases'], defaultNS: 'common'. Access with t('cases:title') or useTranslation('cases')

best_practices.statement: Organize namespaces by feature, not by type. Use common namespace for shared translations. Keep namespaces focused and cohesive. Typical structure: common (buttons, labels), feature namespaces (cases, reports), domain namespaces (auth, validation)

structure.statement: Use hierarchical keys with dot notation: 'cases.list.title', 'auth.login.error'. Benefits: organization, context, easier maintenance. Avoid flat keys like 'casesListTitle'

interpolation.statement: Interpolation inserts dynamic values: 'Hello, {{name}}!'. Use with t('key', { name: 'John' }). Supports nesting: {{user.name}}. Can format: {{value, number}} for numbers

rules.statement: Pluralization uses language-specific rules. English: key_one, key_other. Use with count: t('case', { count: 5 }). i18next automatically selects correct plural form based on language rules

language_specific.statement: Different languages have different plural rules. English: 2 forms (one, other). Russian: 3 forms (one, few, many). Arabic: 6 forms. i18next handles automatically via Intl.PluralRules

strategy.statement: i18next-browser-languagedetector detects language from: querystring (?lng=es), cookie, localStorage, navigator, HTML lang attribute. Configure detection order and caching strategy

configuration.statement: Configure with detection: { order: ['querystring', 'cookie', 'localStorage', 'navigator'], caches: ['localStorage', 'cookie'] }. Caches remember user preference across sessions

structure.statement: Create @company/i18n package with: locales/ (translation files), config.ts (i18next setup), hooks.ts (custom React hooks), components.ts (reusable components). Export everything via index.ts. Benefits: single source of truth, consistent configuration, easy updates

usage.statement: Apps install @company/i18n and import i18n instance: import i18n from '@company/i18n'. Wrap app with I18nextProvider. Use hooks: import { useTranslation } from '@company/i18n'. All translation management centralized

dates.statement: Use Intl.DateTimeFormat for locale-aware dates: new Intl.DateTimeFormat(locale, options).format(date). i18next can integrate via format postprocessor. Example: t('date', { value: date, formatParams: { value: { year: 'numeric', month: 'long' } } })

numbers_currency.statement: Use Intl.NumberFormat for numbers/currency: new Intl.NumberFormat(locale, { style: 'currency', currency: 'USD' }).format(amount). Automatically handles locale-specific separators, decimal points, currency symbols

implementation.statement: RTL (right-to-left) for Arabic, Hebrew, etc. i18n.dir(lng) returns 'rtl' or 'ltr'. Set on document: document.documentElement.setAttribute('dir', i18n.dir()). CSS: html[dir='rtl'] { direction: rtl; }

type_safety.statement: Generate types from translation files: type Translations = typeof import('./locales/en/common.json'). Use with useTranslation<'namespace'>('namespace') for autocomplete. Ensures translation keys exist at compile time

code_splitting.statement: Load translations dynamically with i18next-http-backend: backend: { loadPath: '/locales/{{lng}}/{{ns}}.json' }. Reduces initial bundle size. Loads namespaces on-demand

lazy_loading.statement: Lazy load namespaces with useTranslation('ns', { useSuspense: false }). Returns ready boolean. Show loading state until translations loaded. Reduces initial load time

unit_tests.statement: Test translations by wrapping components in I18nextProvider with test i18n instance. Can change language for testing: await i18n.changeLanguage('es'). Mock translations or use real translation files
